---
title: "FLORA Converter — Notebook Test"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Load the converter

```{r}
source("flora_converter.R")
```

## Option 1 — load from a file path

```{r}
data <- csv_to_flora_json("flora.csv")
cat("Total original papers:", length(data$results))
```

## Option 2 — load from a data.frame you already have

```{r}
df   <- read.csv("flora.csv", fileEncoding = "UTF-8-BOM",
                 stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
data <- csv_to_flora_json(df)
cat("Total original papers:", length(data$results))
```

## Inspect a specific paper

```{r}
results <- data$results
doi     <- "10.1177/0956797610383437"
paper   <- results[[doi]]

cat("Title:  ", paper$title, "\n")
cat("Journal:", paper$journal, "(", paper$year, ")\n")
families <- sapply(paper$authors, `[[`, "family")
cat("Authors:", paste(families, collapse = ", "), "\n")
```

## Replication stats as a data.frame

```{r}
as.data.frame(paper$record$stats)
```

## Replications as a data.frame

```{r}
reps <- paper$record$replications

null_to_na <- function(x, na = NA_character_) if (is.null(x)) na else x

do.call(rbind, lapply(reps, function(r) {
  data.frame(
    title   = null_to_na(r$title),
    year    = null_to_na(r$year, NA_integer_),
    journal = null_to_na(r$journal),
    outcome = null_to_na(r$outcome),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))
```
